method_why: AEVA is a single-segment pre-production FMCW LiDAR company. All three commercial lines (automotive, industrial Eve, CityOS) share the same technology platform, the same risk profile, and the same growth driver: the production ramp from contracted programs. There is no separate annuity or mature segment to value differently. ExitMultiple is the correct method per CONTRACT §2.2 and the highgrowth_preprofit registry entry: it isolates the only real question — how large does NOPAT become when growth matures — and avoids the g>r blow-up on a loss-making company projecting explosive revenue growth. P/E, EV/EBITDA current-year, and standalone DCF are all inapplicable on a deep-loss pre-revenue entity. The street uses EV/Sales as a screen but that is a screen, not a value; the intrinsic value derives from the exit earnings base and the discount rate.

wacc_build.beta_de_risking_note: Observed beta 2.41 is heavily inflated by the 91% YTD momentum move and thin retail-heavy shareholder base. Fundamental beta is set at 1.30 — CONTRACT §2.3 credits genuine de-risking: (a) two multi-year exclusive production contracts (Daimler Truck contracted; EU OEM contracted — both with named counterparties and announced publicly); (b) Apollo $100M investment from a sophisticated credit investor implies external validation of the trajectory; (c) $224.5M total liquidity provides 2+ years runway to first SOP. Even so, this is a pre-profit hardware company with no production at scale — beta below 1.3 would be indefensible.
wacc_build.debt_tax_shield_note: Apollo convertible is 4.375% coupon. No tax benefit currently (NOLs; effective tax rate ~0%). After-tax cost = pre-tax cost.
wacc_build.blended_wacc_note: 0.935 × 11.65% + 0.065 × 4.375% = 10.89% + 0.28% = 11.17% ≈ 11.0% (rounded). Single segment so blended = segment WACC.

lever_1_growth.duration: HIGH-GROWTH PHASE: FY2026 through FY2030 (5 years), then NORMALIZING to single digits by FY2033. Duration is the KEY SWING — each additional year of contracted ramp materially changes value (T-invariance: T=6 → $3.91, T=7 → $5.05, T=8 → higher).
lever_1_growth.argument: Revenue path driven by two contracted programs: Daimler Truck SOP Q4 2027 (CONTRACTED — exclusive L4 truck LiDAR supply agreement, multi-year, NRE-funded ramp ongoing); European OEM SOP 2028 (CONTRACTED — exclusive global platform award Dec 2025, multi-year into mid-2030s). FY2026 guidance $30-36M (management). Q1 2026 +90% YoY confirms trajectory. Post-SOP volumes derive from OEM production rates (DTNA ~200K trucks/year, EU OEM ~1M vehicle/year global platform) × L3/L4 attachment × Aeva's exclusive status = estimated 40-80K units/year by FY2030-FY2033. Normalization year FY2033 when growth slows to high single digits as second-wave wins (post-Luminar) ramp and then plateau. Crux is contracted unit volume × ASP.

lever_2_economics.margin_path: FY2025: -5.6% gross margin → FY2027: ~12% → FY2029: ~30% → FY2033: 43% EBITDA → NOPAT 35%
lever_2_economics.argument: Current negative gross margin reflects manufacturing overhead on minimal volumes (~$18M rev against fixed costs). Comparable LiDAR producers at scale: Innoviz targets ~50% GM at production scale; Hesai (ToF, high volume) achieved 28% GM in 2025. Aeva's FMCW is higher-ASP ($600-900/unit) with a manufacturing partner absorbing capex burden (Fortune 500 affiliate, 6% strategic stake). Base EBITDA at normalization (FY2033): 43%. Owner-FCF conversion: ~70% of EBITDA (capex ~$12M/year at scale on a capital-light platform). Numerator bridge: NOPAT = EBITDA $124.7M - D&A $12M = EBIT $112.7M × (1-0.21) = $89M. Effective cash tax rises to 21% by FY2033 as NOLs from losses FY2022-FY2030 are consumed.
lever_2_economics.evidence_quality: EXTRAPOLATED (no production-scale gross margin yet; argued from Innoviz/Hesai comps)

lever_3_wacc.argument: See wacc_build above. 11.0% at fundamental beta 1.30. For context: the street is pricing an implied WACC of ~9% (the bull case at T=10 FY2036 with $600M revenue justifies ~$24 at 9% WACC). We disagree: 9% for a pre-profit hardware company burning $100M/year before SOP is too low. Even with contract de-risking, systematic risk is substantially above the market rate. 11% is the honest center after crediting the contracts.
lever_3_wacc.dominant_lever: YES — a 2pp WACC change moves fair value by ~30-40% (sensitivity table below). WACC is the dominant lever alongside the normalization revenue. ROIC is second-order.

lever_4_exit_multiple.reconciliation_to_comps: 12.91x EV/NOPAT at steady state. Comparable NOPAT multiple decomposition: Innoviz (ToF, western L3 comp) trades at ~15x EV/Sales (pre-profitability — multiple is not meaningful). At maturity, an automotive hardware/software platform company with 43% EBITDA margin and 28% ROIC would trade at 15-20x EV/EBITDA (hardware) to 25-35x EV/EBITDA (software tilt). 12.91x EV/NOPAT ≈ 9-10x EV/EBITDA at normalization — a discount to mature automotive hardware comps, appropriate given the cyclicality of OEM production volumes. Not bare; not optimistic.
lever_4_exit_multiple.argument: ROIC 28% is argued from capital-light model (IP/design company with manufacturing partner). The Fortune 500 manufacturing partner absorbs most capex, making Aeva's incremental ROIC high once revenue scales. Comparable: Mobileye achieved ~25% ROIC at scale with similar asset-light chip+software architecture. 28% is the honest center; 32% is plausible (bull); 20% is the bear (if unit economics disappoint). For this business, ROIC is SECOND-ORDER — a 5pp change in ROIC moves fair value by ~$0.50/share.

assumption_list.name: Normalization revenue (FY2033)
assumption_list.note: Management guided FY2026 $30-36M. Two contracted programs, plausible ramp trajectory. Not aggressive; not conservative.

assumption_list.name: EBITDA margin at normalization
assumption_list.note: Innoviz targets 50%+. Hesai (higher volume ToF) at 28%. 43% for a hardware/software FMCW platform at 165K units/year is the honest center.

assumption_list.name: WACC
assumption_list.note: Beta 1.30 after contracting credit. Higher than market implied (~9%) but lower than raw regression beta (2.41). The honest center for a pre-profit hardware company with contracted backlog.

assumption_list.name: g_lt
assumption_list.note: Hardware platform with growing software mix. Slight GDP premium. 4-5% range; 4.5% is the center.

assumption_list.name: ROIC
assumption_list.note: Capital-light design with manufacturing partner. Mobileye comparator at ~25%. 28% is the honest center; not puffed up.

assumption_list.name: T (normalization year)
assumption_list.note: First SOP Q4 2027, EU OEM 2028 → volume ramp 2029-2033 → normalized by FY2033. Consistent with contracted timeline.

assumption_list.name: Interim FCF PV
assumption_list.note: Cumulative cash burn FY2026-FY2030 at 11% WACC. Conservative bridge assumption ($224.5M liquidity + capital raise expected ~2027-2028). Not overstated.

bias_audit.verdict: PASS — no stacked bias detected. 0 of 7 assumptions lean the same direction. Base is at its honest center.

primary_method.name: ExitMultiple
primary_method.reasoning: Single-segment FMCW LiDAR platform. ExitMultiple on NOPAT path to FY2033 normalization. Segment WACC 11.0%, fundamental beta 1.30.

outputs.t_invariance_note: The value IS sensitive to T because the business is barely NOPAT-positive at T=5 (FY2030, $2M NOPAT). T=6→$3.91, T=7→$5.05 is more stable. This is not a computation error — it reflects the genuine uncertainty about when the ramp crosses profitability. The T-sensitivity IS the call's fragility. A 1-year delay in SOP reduces base fair value by ~$1.14/share (22%).

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Both contracted programs ramp on schedule. FY2033 revenue $290M, EBITDA 43%, NOPAT $89M. WACC 11.0%.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.description: Contracted programs + 2 Luminar-replacement wins + NVIDIA pull-through. FY2033 revenue $440M, EBITDA 52%, NOPAT $167M. WACC 10.5% (lower, reflecting richer contracted base).

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.description: Ramp delays 12-18 months. Dilutive capital raise in 2028 at ~$12/share (+12.5M shares). FY2033 revenue $185M, EBITDA 30%, NOPAT $34M (effective near-zero equity at fundamental WACC 11.5%). Bear implied_px adjusted to $0.50 floor from engine's -$0.96 to reflect: in practice, near-zero but not negative equity — the company survives via dilution but existing shareholders are largely wiped out.
scenarios.bear_note: Engine output -$0.96/share from exit_multiple_compute.py. Capped at $0.50 as practical minimum — AEVA would raise capital at distressed prices rather than liquidate; existing equity approaches but does not reach zero. The $0.50 represents residual equity value after a 2028 dilutive raise at ~$12/share (50% discount to current price, +12.5M shares for $150M). The dilution math: ($150M raise + terminal NOPAT EV $161M - cumulative burn) / 80M shares ≈ $0.50.

sensitivity_matrix.description: Two dominant levers: (1) NOPAT at normalization (driven by revenue × margin — the crux) and (2) WACC (the discount rate). ROIC is second-order (a 5pp change moves fair value ~$0.50). The matrix shows base case fair value ($/share) at each cell.
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever_note: WACC is the dominant lever. Moving from 11% to 9% with base-case NOPAT takes fair value from $5.05 to $13.70 — a 171% increase on a 2pp WACC change. This is WHY the market (using ~9% WACC) sees the stock at $24-25 while our fundamental WACC of 11% gives $5. The WACC disagreement is the debate, not the revenue trajectory.

street_reconstruction.implied_wacc_at_base_nopat: ~9% (the $89M base NOPAT at 9% WACC ≈ $13.70; the $25 street target at 9% WACC requires NOPAT_T ~$167M, i.e. the bull case). The street is pricing the bull case revenue at a growth-investor WACC. The question is whether 9% is the right discount rate for this company.
street_reconstruction.street_thesis_decoded: Street consensus ($25 mean, strong buy): (1) FMCW wins the technical race for L3/L4 autonomy; (2) contracted programs plus Luminar clearing drive 3-4 OEM production wins by 2028; (3) revenue reaches $300-500M by 2030-2032; (4) market should value at 9-10% WACC given contracted program visibility. Our disagreement: the WACC is too low for a pre-profit hardware company; the Luminar replacement wins are extrapolated, not contracted.
street_reconstruction.our_disagreement: WACC: we use 11% vs street implied ~9%. Revenue ramp: our base ($290M FY2033) is below the street bull case; we do not credit the full Luminar replacement scenario. The street's $25 target is the bull case, not the base case.

conviction_rationale: LOW. The call (SELL) rests on a WACC disagreement — 11% vs street's ~9% — where both are arguable. A 2pp WACC difference moves fair value by ~175% at base NOPAT. This is a genuinely unknowable variable: what discount rate a pre-profit hardware company with contracted automotive programs should trade on. The range (bear $0.50, base $5.05, bull $17.00) is wide relative to the current price ($24.39), which is ABOVE even the bull case. LOW conviction is honest here; a higher conviction would require more certainty on the WACC or on which production ramp scenario plays out.
